page: Microsoft Azure E48as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It includes 786 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Multi-threaded decompression and image processing also demonstrate strong performance. However, memory bandwidth for large block reads and writes remains in the average tier. Large language model inference for a 70B model is a notable bottleneck, with text generation speeds falling into the bottom 25%.

The E48as_v4 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. The hardware profile is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture using Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance includes 786 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded decompression, positioning it in the top 25% for parallel processing tasks. Conversely, memory bandwidth and large-scale LLM text generation perform in the average and weak tiers, respectively. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core makes this server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy operations without GPU requirements. It is suited for database hosting, in-memory analytics, and enterprise application workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v4 features 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 7763 processors, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring high memory density and strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores.
Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and 786 GB of local storage.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks.
Average memory bandwidth and weak large-scale LLM text generation speeds.
Suited for databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
